Pegma Omni-4

An all-in-one aeroponic growing system combining an adjustable rack, 800 W full-spectrum LED, four high-performance aeroponic buckets, flexible stage-specific ventilation, and optional IoT integration — engineered for consistent, high-quality cannabis cultivation within grow tents. 1 m² base footprint, extendable to 2.15 m.

Pegma Omni-4

Everything You Need.
Ready to Grow from Day One.

Adjustable rack, 800 W LED, four aeroponic buckets, flexible ventilation, and touchscreen control — all included. Designed for grow tent environments, 1 m² footprint.

Overview

The Complete Aeroponic Grow Kit

The Pegma Omni-4 ships as a complete, ready-to-deploy system — every component required for professional aeroponic cannabis cultivation is included in the box. Designed specifically for grow tent environments, it delivers efficient root-zone nutrient delivery and spectrum-optimised lighting from seedling through flower, with ventilation that adapts at each stage.

Kit Components

Six Integrated Systems in One Kit

Every element of the Omni-4 is purpose-designed to work together. The following components are included in the complete kit as shipped.

Precision Aeroponic Buckets

20–60 μm Mist. Maximum Root Uptake.

The Omni-4's four aeroponic buckets suspend roots in air and deliver precision-size nutrient droplets (20–60 μm) directly to root hairs. This droplet range maximises surface contact with root hairs while maintaining high oxygen availability — outperforming deep-water culture and coco substrates for root development speed.

A 60 L reservoir with visible level tube and included water pump keeps nutrient supply consistent. Nutrient management is manual mode for full grower control.

🏗️
Adjustable Growing Rack
Light-integrated aluminium rack with 1 m² base footprint. Height-adjustable from 600 mm to 1,300 mm maximum growing height to track plant development across all three growth stages.

Adjustable Exhaust Duct System
Stage-specific ventilation with expandable and swappable exhaust ducts. Growers swap duct configurations at each growth stage to optimise internal air dynamics — without replacing the unit.

Flexible Ventilation Control

Stage-Adaptive Airflow for Every Growth Phase

The Omni-4's adjustable exhaust duct system is engineered for the specific airflow requirements of each cannabis growth stage. Swapping the duct configuration takes minutes — no tools, no rewiring. This is the key differentiator that separates the Omni-4 from fixed-vent growing systems.

Seedling Stage
Vegetative Stage
Flowering Stage
Stage 1
Seedling
Standard Exhaust Duct
Gentle, reduced-pressure airflow prevents mechanical stress on young stems and root development. Maintains stable humidity and temperature to support rapid establishment without transpiration shock.
Stage 2
Vegetative
Shortened Duct Extension
Increased plant height and enhanced transpiration rates demand improved mid-canopy airflow. The shortened duct extension redirects air movement upward through the expanding canopy — promoting vigorous lateral leaf development and preventing humidity stratification.
Stage 3
Flowering
Upper Canopy Directional
Directs airflow through the upper canopy where flower clusters form — reducing localised humidity and heat stress around developing buds. Critical for preventing botrytis and supporting optimal terpene and cannabinoid development at harvest.
Growing Lifecycle

From Seedling to Harvest

🌱
Phase 1
Seedling
600 mm growing height. Standard exhaust duct. Gentle airflow. Light at low intensity on full-spectrum. Root zone misting initiates early root-hair development.
🌿
Phase 2
Vegetative
Rack extends to accommodate canopy height. Shortened duct extension fitted. LED intensity increased. Irrigation cycles adjusted for higher root demand. Full nutrient programme active.
🌸
Phase 3
Flowering
Up to 1,300 mm maximum growing height. Upper-canopy directional airflow. Lighting transitioned to flowering spectrum programme. Nutrient formulation adjusted for reproductive-stage requirements.

FAQ

Common Questions

What size grow tent does the Pegma Omni-4 fit?

The Omni-4 has a 1 m² (1,000 × 1,000 mm) base footprint, making it compatible with all standard 1 × 1 m grow tents and larger. The frame extends to 2,155 mm total height — verify your tent's interior height accommodates this before ordering.

How does the aeroponic bucket system work?

Each of the four aeroponic buckets contains precision nozzles that mist nutrient solution at 20–60 μm droplet size directly onto exposed root zones. This droplet range maximises surface-area contact with root hairs without submerging them — sustaining high oxygen availability throughout the root zone and accelerating nutrient uptake compared to deep-water culture or coco methods.

Why does the ventilation system change between growth stages?

Airflow requirements differ substantially across the cannabis growth cycle. Young seedlings need gentle, humid air with minimal mechanical stress. Vegetative plants transpire heavily and need more mid-canopy airflow to prevent hot spots and humidity build-up. Flowering plants require directional airflow through the upper canopy to reduce botrytis risk around dense bud sites. The swappable duct system enables growers to precisely match ventilation to plant physiology at each stage.

What is the difference between the standard and shortened exhaust duct?

The standard duct is used during the seedling stage — it maintains gentler airflow with reduced pressure. The shortened duct extension increases plant height clearance during vegetative and flowering stages, directing air upward through the growing canopy. Both ducts are included in the kit; switching takes minutes and requires no tools.

Is IoT and SaaS connectivity available?

Yes — IoT and SaaS integration is available as an optional upgrade. When connected, the system enables remote monitoring, data logging, and cultivation programme management via the GrowSpec cloud platform. The 7-inch touchscreen control functions fully as a standalone controller without connectivity if preferred.

What is the power requirement for the Omni-4?

The system requires a 220–240 V, 50–60 Hz supply with a peak draw of 900 W. The LED alone draws 800 W at full power. The LED input accepts 100–277 V AC, providing flexibility across international electrical standards. All power distribution components (power box, adapter) are included in the kit.

Professional aeroponic cultivation, complete from day one.

Contact our team to discuss the Pegma Omni-4 for your grow tent setup — including yield planning, ventilation advice, and pricing.
